methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Pre-Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $2M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Drones & Autonomous (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 4 same-sector peers exist in Drones & Autonomous, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
DroneForge is a pre-seed stage company in the Drones & Autonomous sector founded by Chester Zelaya. The company has raised $2.5M to date. The relevant comp universe consists of four private peers in the sector—Joby Aviation, Nuro, Archer Aviation, and Elroy Air—with no public comparables available. Due to the small and heterogeneous peer set, same-stage and adjacent-stage filtering yielded insufficient datapoints for a meaningful peer-median valuation approach.

The Stage-Progression method is applicable and serves as the primary valuation anchor. Using total capital raised ($2.5M) as a proxy for current implied value and applying a typical Pre-Seed to next-stage step-up multiplier of 2.5x yields an implied valuation of $6M. This method reflects the historical trajectory of capital deployment and milestone achievement expected between pre-seed and seed/Series A stages within the sector.

Triangulation on a single method yields a median valuation of $6M with a range of $5M to $7M. The low-end estimate reflects downside risk inherent in pre-seed stage companies; the high-end reflects upside to the step-up multiplier if the company achieves notable technical or commercial milestones before the next fundraise. Key uncertainties include the company's actual product progress, customer traction, and the broader fundraising environment for autonomous systems. A peer-median approach should be revisited once same-stage comparables with disclosed valuations emerge.
